column1: # ZAP scan rules file
# Format: TSV with the following columns (tab-separated):
#   rule_id   action   url_regex   parameter   evidence   confidence   risk
# Columns:
#   rule_id     — ZAP alert/plugin ID (integer). Use 0 to match all rules.
#   action      — IGNORE (suppress entirely), WARN (report but don't fail), FAIL (fail the scan).
#   url_regex   — Regular expression matched against the request URL. Use .* to match all URLs.
#   parameter   — Request parameter name to match (leave blank to match any parameter).
#   evidence    — Evidence string to match in the alert (leave blank to match any evidence).
#   confidence  — Confidence level to match: Low, Medium, High, or blank to match all.
#   risk        — Risk level to match: Informational, Low, Medium, High, or blank to match all.
# Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom. First match wins.
# Lines starting with # are comments and are ignored.
# Reference: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/desktop/addons/automation-framework/job-activescan/#alertfilters
# 10027 — Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments
# False positive: ZAP flags TODO/FIXME comments in JS source maps and minified
# bundles. These are development artefacts, not sensitive disclosures.
# 10096 — Timestamp Disclosure
# Warn rather than fail: timestamps in API responses (e.g. created_at, updated_at)
# are expected and intentional. Review findings manually before suppressing further.
# 90022 — Application Error Disclosure
# Warn rather than fail: some health-check and status endpoints intentionally
# surface non-sensitive error states. Review each finding before ignoring.
# 10021 — X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
# Warn rather than fail: this header should be set but its absence is low risk
# on API-only services that never serve HTML. Fix at the framework/middleware
# level rather than suppressing.
